mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. The key trade-off is that it runs entirely in Docker, which may not fully replicate a production environment.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the stable binary for your platform.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 architecture
> For ARM64 architecture
> Make the Kind binary executable
> Move Kind to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up the kubeconfig file to interact with the Kind cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path)

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the Kind cluster's kubeconfig

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the cluster nodes are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Should show the nodes in the Kind cluster

uninstall.title: Remove the Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Note that any workloads running in the cluster will be lost.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> Deletes the Kind cluster

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backed up any important data. This step upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Download the latest Kind binary
> Make the Kind binary executable
> Replace the old Kind binary with the new one

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that /usr/local/bin is in your PATH or move the Kind binary to a directory that is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start the control plane'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure you have at least 2 vCPUs and 4GB of RAM allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp ...: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind cluster is not running or kubeconfig is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'kind get kubeconfig-path' and ensure KUBECONFIG is set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes version specified is not supported by Kind.

**Fix:**
Check the supported Kubernetes versions and use a valid version.

**Affected versions:** <= v0.31.0

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved error messages.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
